stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and performance tracking for a major LLM model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate its relevance within the transformers and safetensors ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture and uses standard ML formats like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI, indicating its primary function.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating an upcoming performance boost for local AI deployment.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy and control.

stories.title: White House drastically shortens deadline for dropping quantum-vulnerable crypto
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: The White House has accelerated the deadline for transitioning to quantum-resistant cryptography.
The urgency stems from national security concerns regarding current crypto systems being vulnerable to quantum computing attacks.
This signals a major, government-mandated shift in cybersecurity infrastructure adoption.

stories.title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Oracle is undertaking massive, debt-fueled investments in data center infrastructure to support its AI initiatives.
The company is implementing significant workforce reductions (21,000 layoffs) concurrently with its major AI spending push.
The story highlights the financial strategy of large tech firms balancing operational costs (layoffs) with aggressive future investment (AI infrastructure).

stories.title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
stories.author: Mac Schwager
bullet_points: Introduces 'InSight,' a framework designed to overcome the limitations of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models in skill acquisition.
The core innovation is making VLAs 'steerable' at the primitive-action level (e.g., specific actions like 'lift upward').
The framework uses an automated segmentation pipeline to partition demonstrations into labeled, actionable primitives.

stories.title: FLUX3D: High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Generation with Diffusion-Aligned Sparse Representation
stories.author: Hengkai Guo
bullet_points: FLUX3D introduces a new method for high-fidelity 3D Gaussian generation.
It addresses limitations in current sparse voxel representations used for image-to-3DGS, specifically detail loss.
The method tackles bottlenecks by moving beyond discriminative 2D features and improving diffusion transformer integration.

stories.title: OpenThoughts-Agent: Data Recipes for Agentic Models
stories.author: Ludwig Schmidt
bullet_points: Addresses the critical gap in training data curation for broadly capable agentic AI models.
Introduces OpenThoughts-Agent (OT-Agent), a fully open data curation pipeline for these models.
Moves beyond single-benchmark training efforts (like SWE-Smith or SERA) to promote generalization across diverse agentic tasks.

stories.title: IV-CoT: Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought for Structure-Aware Text-to-Image Generation
stories.author: Zhenan Sun
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in current Text-to-Image models: poor structure-aware prompt following (e.g., object counts, spatial relations).
Proposes IV-CoT (Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought) to decouple structural planning from appearance rendering.
Aims to improve the fidelity and controllability of unified multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) in image generation.

stories.title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
stories.author: Mac Schwager
bullet_points: Introduces 'InSight,' a framework designed to enable autonomous skill acquisition in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.
Addresses the limitation of existing VLAs by making them 'steerable' at the primitive-action level (e.g., 'lift upward').
The framework utilizes an automated segmentation pipeline to partition demonstrations into labeled, actionable primitives.

stories.title: Grad Detect: Gradient-Based Hallucination Detection in LLMs
stories.author: Brent M. Werness
bullet_points: Introduces 'Grad Detect,' a novel gradient-based method for detecting hallucinations in Large Language Models (LLMs).
The technique analyzes layer-wise gradient patterns during a single forward-backward pass at inference time.
Addresses the critical need for reliable LLM deployment in high-stakes applications by mitigating hallucination risks.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, particularly benefiting users on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has announced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: World Models in Pieces: Structural Certification for General Agents
stories.author: Tongxin Li
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard guarantees for general AI agents operating in complex environments ('big-world regime').
It proves that general agents cannot be universally capable, invalidating traditional worst-case analysis.
A new methodology, 'structural certification,' is introduced as a transition-local framework to certify agent capabilities.

stories.title: Grading the Grader: Lessons from Evaluating an Agentic Data Analysis System
stories.author: Kai-Tai Hsu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the complex evaluation of agentic data analysis systems, which produce multi-faceted outputs (code, results, diagnostics).
A key challenge identified is distinguishing genuine output disagreement from artifacts introduced by automated grading methods.
The research investigates automated grader reliability and proposes strategies, utilizing a multi-agent system (LAMBDA), to improve grading quality.

stories.title: Accuracy and Satisfaction in Multi-Turn LLM Dialogues for NFR Assessment
stories.author: Sepideh Ghanavati
bullet_points: Current LLM evaluation benchmarks primarily focus on functional correctness, neglecting conversational quality.
There is a critical gap in assessing LLM dialogue assistants' ability to handle Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs).
The research proposes new methods needed to evaluate collaborative reasoning about vague and context-dependent NFRs.

stories.title: Real vs. Complex Spectral Bases for Neural Operators: The Role of Green's Function Alignment
stories.author: Sathya Ravi
bullet_points: Introduces the Hartley Neural Operator (HNO) as a real-valued alternative to Fourier Neural Operators (FNO).
HNO replaces the complex Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) with the purely real Discrete Hartley Transform.
This change aims to eliminate representational redundancy inherent in complex FFTs when dealing with real-valued PDE solutions.

stories.title: A Physics-Informed Fourier-Wavelet Transformer for Multiscale Computational Fluid Dynamics Surrogate Modeling
stories.author: Xizhong Chen
bullet_points: Introduces a Physics-Informed Fourier-Wavelet Transformer for CFD surrogate modeling.
Addresses a limitation in existing methods by improving the reproduction of localized multiscale flow structures.
Combines hybrid Fourier-wavelet spectral encoding with physics-biased self-attention for velocity-field reconstruction.

stories.title: FlowPipe: LLM-Enhanced Conditional Generative Flow Networks for Data Preparation Pipeline Construction
stories.author: Yanwei Yu
bullet_points: Introduces FlowPipe, an LLM-enhanced method for automatically constructing data preparation pipelines.
Addresses the computational difficulty of pipeline construction due to the combinatorial nature of operator sequences.
Overcomes limitations of existing Multi-DQN methods, specifically concerning long-horizon credit assignment in sequential tasks.

stories.title: Less is More: Quality-Aware Training Data Selection for Scientific Summarization
stories.author: Grigorios Tsoumakas
bullet_points: Addresses the issue of low quality and inconsistent alignment of author-written abstracts used as gold standards in scientific summarization.
Presents a solution by constructing and releasing one of the largest biomedical and life science datasets for long-document summarization.
Contributes to improving long-context models by providing a larger and more structured dataset for scientific summarization tasks.

stories.title: SHERLOC: Structured Diagnostic Localization for Code Repair Agents
stories.author: Boris Ginsburg
bullet_points: Introduces SHERLOC, a new training-free framework designed for structured diagnostic localization in code repair.
Addresses a key limitation in current LLM-based code repair agents: excessive budget spent on fault location without proper diagnostic context.
Combines a reasoning LLM with compact representations to move beyond simple file retrieval toward actionable diagnosis.

stories.title: Paying to Know: Micro-Transaction Markets for Verified Product Information in Agentic E-Commerce
stories.author: Matthew Shardlow
bullet_points: The paper proposes a shift in e-commerce focus from product matching to information acquisition in agentic systems.
It argues that autonomous buyer agents make exhaustive product investigation possible, changing the bottleneck.
The solution envisioned is a micro-transaction market where users pay for trustworthy, decision-relevant product information.

stories.title: Are We Ready For An Agent-Native Memory System?
stories.author: Fan Wu
bullet_points: LLM agent memory is evolving beyond simple retrieval to complex data management systems supporting storage, retrieval, and governance.
Current evaluations often treat agent memory as a monolithic black box, relying only on end-to-end task success metrics (e.g., F1, BLEU).
The paper suggests a need for more granular evaluation methods to properly assess the capabilities of underlying agent memory systems.

stories.title: Posterior Refinement: Fast Language Generation via Any-Order Flow Maps
stories.author: Nicholas M. Boffi
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in non-autoregressive language generation, specifically the factorization error in Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs).
It introduces a method leveraging Flow Map Language Models (FMLMs) to enable iterative refinement and simultaneous token generation.
The core concept is 'Posterior Refinement,' allowing models to recursively critique and regenerate subsets of tokens for improved sample quality.

stories.title: Haystack: Open-Source AI Framework for Production Ready Agents, RAG
stories.author: doener
bullet_points: Haystack is an open-source AI framework designed for building production-ready AI agents.
The framework specifically supports Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) workflows.
Its release targets the practical application and deployment of advanced AI systems.

stories.title: Everyone's Excited About Claude Tag. Nobody's Built the Trust Layer.
stories.author: dannwaneri
bullet_points: The article discusses 'Claude Tag,' a topic generating significant excitement in the tech community.
It highlights a perceived gap in the current ecosystem: the lack of a dedicated 'Trust Layer' for such technologies.
The discussion involves high-profile figures, referencing Andrej Karpathy (OpenAI co-founder).

stories.title: Auto-verifying your AI-SRE's fixes (Part II): HolmesGPT end-to-end on a real cluster
stories.author: eyalb
bullet_points: The article details the testing of HolmesGPT, an AI-SRE tool, in a live GKE cluster.
The AI was used to automatically verify fixes for planted bugs, demonstrating end-to-end capability.
The system successfully passed one fix and correctly rejected another, indicating functional validation.
